title: rhamnus purshiana var. tomentella
intro: Rhamnus purshiana var. tomentella, also known as cascara sagrada, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anthraquinone glycosides that stimulate bowel movements and have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of cascara sagrada include relief from constipation, irritable bowel syndrome, and other digestive issues, as well as potential antiviral and antifungal properties. Cascara sagrada is commonly prepared as a dried bark, tea, or tincture, and is also used in herbal supplements in the form of capsules or tablets.
